seo.title: Pennsylvania Dental Assistant License (2026) | Fees & Guide
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Pennsylvania — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license pennsylvania, dental assistant certification pennsylvania, RDA pennsylvania, DANB CDA pennsylvania, dental assistant radiography pennsylvania

board.name: Pennsylvania State Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address:
Pennsylvania State Board of Dentistry
P.O. Box 2649
Harrisburg, PA 17105-2649

license_types.title: Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Pennsylvania.

steps.title: Create an Account on PALS
steps.description: Navigate to the PALS - Pennsylvania Licensing System website and register for a new user account if you do not already have one. This account will be used to manage all aspects of your application.

steps.title: Start the Initial EFDA Application
steps.description: Log into your PALS account, locate the 'Board of Dentistry' section, and select the application for 'Expanded Function Dental Assistant' initial licensure. Complete all required sections of the online application form.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation and Pay Fee
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents through PALS, which typically include official transcripts from a Board-approved EFDA program, proof of passing the Pennsylvania EFDA examination administered by DANB, and current CPR certification. Pay the non-refundable application fee via PALS.

steps.title: Complete Child Abuse Recognition and Reporting Training
steps.description: Ensure you have completed the mandatory 3-hour training on Child Abuse Recognition and Reporting from a Board-approved provider. Proof of completion, usually in the form of a certificate, must be submitted or attested to within your PALS application.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: Once your complete application and all supporting documentation have been submitted through PALS, the Pennsylvania State Board of Dentistry will review your qualifications. Upon approval, your Expanded Function Dental Assistant license will be issued.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Pennsylvania State Board of Dentistry by certification.

faqs.question: Does Pennsylvania require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pennsylvania requires Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Pennsylvania.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $75. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Pennsylvania State Board of Dentistry by certification.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Pennsylvania
hero.dek: In Pennsylvania, general Dental Assistants are not licensed; however, Expanded Function Dental Assistants (EFDAs) must apply for licensure through the PALS - Pennsylvania Licensing System.

official_links.registration_steps: New users must first register for an account on the PALS system by clicking 'Register a New Account' and following the prompts to create a user ID and password. Once registered, log in to access the application portal.
